"  
Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth-degree polynomial term and an absolute value term. The absolute value term ensures that the function is symmetric about the y-axis. This symmetry implies that if a solution exists for a positive value of x, there will be a corresponding solution for the negative value of x. Therefore, the number of solutions will be even unless there is a solution at x = 0.]  

Sec. 2  
[To determine the number of real solutions, consider the behavior of the function for positive and negative x values. For x ≥ 0, the equation simplifies to a polynomial equation. Analyzing this equation reveals that it has two real solutions. Due to the symmetry of the absolute value term, there will also be two real solutions for x < 0. Thus, the equation has a total of four real solutions.]  
"
